revision.text:
[[ja:朝倉古墳]][[zh:朝倉古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site | name = Asakura Kofun | image = [[File:Asakura Kofun, entrance-1-2.jpg|280px]]
Entrance to the stone chamber | location = {{ill|Kōchi, Kōchi|en|Kōchi, Kōchi|qid=Q200251|lt=Kōchi, Kōchi|1=Kōchi, Kōchi}}, {{ill|Kōchi Prefecture|en|Kōchi Prefecture|qid=Q134093|lt=Kōchi Prefecture|1=Kōchi Prefecture}}, Japan{{Sfn|Survey Report on Asakura Kofun|2005|qq=}} | coordinates = {{Coord|33.553197|N|133.477797|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = Unknown | Burial Facility = Double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} | built = Early {{ill|7th century|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/7th century|qq=}}|burial goods = (Reportedly) horse tack, {{ill|iron arrowheads|ja|鉄鏃|1=Iron arrowheads|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/iron arrowheads|13=User:Immanuelle/Iron arrowheads|qq=}}, {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|qid=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}} | historical_site_Historic Site = Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site "Asakura Kofun" | map = Japan Kochi }} '''Asakura Kofun''' (朝倉古墳) is an ancient {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|qid=Q1141225|lt=Kofun|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Kōchi, Kōchi|en|Kōchi, Kōchi|qid=Q200251|lt=Kōchi, Kōchi|1=Kōchi, Kōchi}}, {{ill|Kōchi Prefecture|en|Kōchi Prefecture|qid=Q134093|lt=Kōchi Prefecture|1=Kōchi Prefecture}}, Japan. It has been Historic Site1 as a historic site by Kōchi Prefecture. Together with {{ill|Koren Kofun|lt=Koren Kofun|lt=Kohasu Kofun|ja|小蓮古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kohasu Kofun|qq=}} in {{ill|Nankoku, Kōchi|en|Nankoku, Kōchi|qid=Q861621|lt=Nankoku, Kōchi|1=Nankoku, Kōchi}} and {{ill|Myoken Hikoyama Kofun Group|ja|明見彦山古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myoken Hikoyama Kofun Group|qq=}} No. 1 Kofun, it is referred to as one of the "Three Great Kofun of Tosa." == Overview == Asakura Kofun is situated at the southern base of Mount Aka-gi (赤鬼山), the sacred mountain of {{ill|Asakura Shrine (Kōchi)|ja|朝倉神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asakura Shrine (Kōchi)|qq=}} in western Kōchi Plain. The Kofun's soil cover has been removed since early in the Showa period due to land clearing, leaving the stone chamber exposed. Several excavation surveys have been conducted. The tumulus' type is uncertain. The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} with an overall length of slightly over 10 meters. burial goods such as horse tack, {{ill|iron arrowheads|ja|鉄鏃|1=Iron arrowheads|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/iron arrowheads|13=User:Immanuelle/Iron arrowheads|qq=}}, and {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|qid=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}} were reportedly excavated in the early Meiji period, although the current whereabouts of these items are unknown. The chamber's construction features massive stones, indicative of the late {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|qid=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}}, around the early 7th century. The site was Historic Site1 as a Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site in 1950. == History == * Early Meiji period: Investigation of the stone chamber. burial goods including armor, horse tack, {{ill|iron arrowheads|ja|鉄鏃|1=Iron arrowheads|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/iron arrowheads|13=User:Immanuelle/Iron arrowheads|qq=}}, and {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|qid=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}} reportedly discovered. * 1930 (Showa 5): Mound survey conducted by a youth association. * 1935 (Showa 10): Investigation of the stone chamber. {{ill|Iron arrowheads|ja|鉄鏃|1=Iron arrowheads|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iron arrowheads|13=User:Immanuelle/Iron arrowheads|qq=}} and fragments of {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|qid=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}} excavated. * 1950 (Showa 25): Designated as a Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site (April 21). * 2004 (Heisei 16): Survey and detailed measurement of the stone chamber by Kōchi University. * 2008 (Heisei 20): Excavation of the burial chamber. * 2009 (Heisei 21): Excavation of the tumulus, passageway, and front courtyard. == burial == [[File:Asakura Kofun, 3D-B-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber|qq=}}]] [[File:Asakura Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber layout|qq=}}]] The burial facility features a double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}. Its area are as follows: * '''Burial Chamber (玄室):''' ** Length: 5.4 meters ** Width: 2.6 meters * '''Entrance Passage (羨道):''' ** Length: 3.9 meters (estimated restored length: 4.9 meters) ** Width: 1.1 meters Asakura Kofun, genshitsu-1-2.jpg|Burial chamber (rear wall view) Asakura Kofun, genshitsu-2-2.jpg|Burial chamber (entrance view) Asakura Kofun, sendou-2-2.jpg|Passageway (entrance view) Asakura Kofun, sendou-1-2.jpg|Passageway (burial chamber view) Asakura Kofun, entrance-2-2.jpg|Entrance to the chamber == Historic Site == === Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site === * '''Historic Site:''' ** '''Asakura Kofun''' – Designated on April 21, 1950.[http://www.kochinet.ed.jp/bunkazai/details/410-2/410-2-02.htm Asakura Kofun] (Kōchi Prefectural Website). == References == {{reflist|qq=}} == Further Reading == * Explanation Board (Installed by Kōchi City Board of Education, 1989) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2005|chapter=|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/9439 |title=Survey Report on Asakura Kofun|publisher=Kōchi University Archaeology Department|ref={{Harv|Survey Report on Asakura Kofun|2005|qq=}}|qq=}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2009|chapter=|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/9754 |title=Summary of Excavations at Asakura Kofun|series=Kōchi University Archaeology Research Series Vol. 6|publisher=Kōchi University Archaeology Department|ref={{Harv|Excavation Summary 2009|2009|qq=}}|qq=}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/9929 |title=Summary of Excavations at Asakura Kofun II|series=Kōchi University Archaeology Research Series Vol. 8|publisher=Kōchi University Archaeology Department|ref={{Harv|Excavation Summary II|2010|qq=}}|qq=}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2012|chapter=|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/10493 |title=Political Relationships of Large Yokoana-style Stone Chambers in the Late Kofun built|series=Kōchi University Archaeology Research Series Vol. 10|publisher=Kōchi University Archaeology Department|ref={{Harv|Excavation Summary III|2012|qq=}}|qq=}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Saburo Kobayashi|authorlink=Saburo Kobayashi (archaeologist)|year=1989|chapter=Asakura Kofun|title=Encyclopedia of Infobox ancient site|publisher=Tokyodo Publishing|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harv|Encyclopedia of Infobox ancient site|1989|qq=}}|qq=}} == See Also == * {{ill|Asakura Shrine (Kōchi)|ja|朝倉神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asakura Shrine (Kōchi)|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Asakura Kofun|qq=}} * [http://www.kochinet.ed.jp/bunkazai/details/410-2/410-2-02.htm Asakura Kofun] – Kōchi Prefectural Website * [http://www.city.kochi.kochi.jp/soshiki/90/cas-pref-2400200.html Asakura Kofun] – Kōchi City Website {{Kofun navbar|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|朝倉古墳|version=103226103|comment=Imported full ja history}}
